script.dialogue: Wow. The marketplace sure is crowded today. Is something going on?
Wait. That sound… Someone’s performing in the square!
♪ Laa-dee-daa-dee-daa… ♪
♪ You brought fire to her frost… And guided her when she was lost… ♪
Oh, that’s Dorothea! She has such a beautiful voice—and she’s so elegant, too! I'd heard she was a big deal in the Empire, but still… So many people have stopped to watch her.
♪ Like wind does billow ivory sails… You’ve reminded me that love prevails… ♪
Dorothea! We love you!
Please, sing us one more song!
Oh, dear. I appreciate all the support, but I really must be off. Thank you all for listening!
Um… Pardon me…
Goodness, these fans really don’t want her to leave, do they? I wonder if there's any way I can help…
I’m actually meeting a classmate right now. We have a big exam to study for. You all understand, right?
Hang on. Is she pointing at me?
Follow my lead.
Some time later…
Ha… I think we lost them…
Phew! That was crazy! Are all opera fans like this?
Thanks so much for playing along, Annette. And sorry for cutting your shopping trip short. I owe you one.
It’s no problem at all! I barely did anything.
Well, I’m grateful regardless. I never intended to attract an audience in the first place, so I’d hate to have inconvenienced anyone.
Uh, based on those cheers, I’d say they were the opposite of inconvenienced. How did you end up in that situation, anyway?
I was strolling by the fountain and spotted a young girl crying. Apparently some of the other kids were making fun of her for being clumsy.
Ugh, that’s awful. Kids can be so mean sometimes.
Yes… They can. I wanted to cheer her up, so I started singing. It was just meant to be for her, but a few people overheard, and… Well, you saw what happened.
Anyway. One of the townsfolk managed to slip me a gift, but I hardly feel like I’ve earned it.
Woah, are those earrings? They’re gorgeous!
They are, aren’t they? But I feel bad receiving something so valuable when I barely sang at all. You, on the other hand, helped me out of an awkward situation, so…
Y-you want me to have them? I couldn’t!
Really, I insist. Those sapphires look like they’d go great with your eyes, after all. And I have more than enough jewelry already.
I mean, if you really don’t want them… Thank you. Should we head back to the monastery?
I think that’d be for the best. I was telling the truth about having an exam to prepare for. I think you might be taking the same one—do you want to go study for it together?
Ooh, yes! It would be an honor! I’d love to compare notes with you…
Dorothea and Annette have reached Support Rank C.
